evals.prompt: ok that's working now. wrap up this session — i'll pick it up in a new chat tomorrow
evals.expected_output: Commits staged specific files (no push if no remote), exports conversation to chat_exports/, writes teaching narrative with the need→journey→result→benefit arc, writes/updates HANDOFF.md, runs the memory-harvest check (may correctly report 'nothing earned it'), closes with a checklist of paths.

evals.prompt: export this conversation to chat_exports/ and add a narrative file that explains the why, what, how and what results came out of it
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as the wrap ritual even though only two steps were named; performs export + narrative and offers/performs the remaining steps (commit, handoff, harvest) rather than stopping at the literal request.

evals.prompt: heading to bed — save whatever you need so we don't lose anything
evals.expected_output: Triggers the full ritual from the session-ending signal. Handoff is high-signal (state, locked decisions, next steps, what-not-to-do). Does not push to any remote without permission.
